mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns in >55% of periods following a 10bps+ rate increase, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: High retail sales growth combined with a rising federal funds rate creates a demand-supply mismatch where strong consumer spending outpaces borrowing costs, fueling corporate revenue expansion and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of triggered months, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production indicates contracting manufacturing output that immediately triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in >65% of months following an INDPRO decline, or the signal fails to predict direction during documented recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean returns in >55% of months following payroll increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.4%, it forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy longer, increasing equity discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >60% of high-inflation months, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for prior rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside expanding industrial production signal synchronized real economy strength that boosts capital goods demand and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction term loses predictive power in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth confirms an economic contraction that immediately reduces corporate earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of quarters following negative GDP growth, or the signal fails to distinguish recessionary quarters from normal cyclical dips.
